libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Target current Pekko 2.0 classic actor APIs such as ActorSystem, Props, AbstractActor, serialization/configuration, and TestKit/TestProbe. Do not use the removed deprecated org.apache.pekko.actor.TypedActor, TypedActorExtension, or TypedProps APIs that existed in 1.1.5. If tests exercise Java serialization, configure pekko.actor.allow-java-serialization = on and disable serializer warnings in the ActorSystem config. If tests exercise DnsSettings/sun.net.dns integration, add --add-exports=java.base/sun.net.dns=ALL-UNNAMED to both JVM test args and native-image build args.

deterministic_setup.reason: Official Pekko Classic Actors module setup includes pekko-testkit in test scope; it provides TestKit/TestProbe utilities needed for reliable actor message and lifecycle assertions.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8742 [Automation] java run fails for org.apache.pekko:pekko-actor_2.13:2.0.0-M1; label=fails-java-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding pekko-testkit broadens the test classpath; tests should use it only as a harness and keep assertions focused on pekko-actor behavior.
The current failure appears to be stale coverage of APIs removed in Pekko 2.0-M1, not a missing dependency.
Pekko 2.0.0-M1 is an experimental milestone and removed a large amount of deprecated code, so API drift from 1.1.5 is likely.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Pekko Actor itself has only scala-library and Typesafe Config as runtime dependencies and needs no Docker service, but the official Classic Actors documentation lists pekko-testkit as the normal test-scoped companion dependency for actor tests.
